book_title: Day Trading and Swing Trading the Currency Market
author: Kathy Lien

strategies.name: Fading the Double Zeros

long: Locate pair trading WELL BELOW its 20-period SMA on 10/15-min chart
Price is approaching a double zero level (e.g., 107.00, 1.2800)
Enter long several pips below the figure (no more than 10 pips below)
short: Locate pair trading WELL ABOVE its 20-period SMA on 10/15-min chart
Price is approaching a double zero level from below
Enter short several pips above the figure (no more than 10 pips above)

exit_rules.take_profit_1: Close half when profit = 2x risk (40 pips if 20-pip stop)
exit_rules.take_profit_2: Trail stop on remainder using 2-bar low/high or 5-bar low/high
exit_rules.breakeven: Move stop to breakeven after first target hit

risk_management.position_sizing: Half position closed at first target, half trailed

strategies.market_conditions: Works best in quieter conditions, no major news catalysts. Tighter range pairs and crosses preferred. Avoid during volatile news releases.
strategies.further_optimization: Higher probability when double zero coincides with key Fibonacci level, moving average, or Bollinger band. Triple zeros (e.g., 1.2000) are even more significant.
strategies.notes: Exploits market microstructure — bank dealers gun stops beyond round numbers, then price reverts. Very practical intraday strategy with tight risk.

strategies.name: Waiting for the Real Deal (London Open Fake-Out)

long: Define range as Frankfurt-to-London power hour (1:00 AM - 2:00 AM NY time)
GBP/USD makes new range low of at least 25 pips below opening price
Pair reverses and penetrates the HIGH of the range
Enter long 10 pips above the high of the range
short: GBP/USD opens in Europe and trades 25+ pips above the high of the power hour range
Pair reverses and penetrates the LOW of the range
Enter short 10 pips below the low of the range

exit_rules.take_profit_2: Trail stop on remainder by 2-bar high/low
exit_rules.breakeven: Move to breakeven after first target

session_rules.trade_window: After U.S. open or after major economic release

strategies.market_conditions: Exploits U.K. dealer stop-hunting behavior at London open. Initial move often false; real move follows. Works best after U.S. open or after major releases.
strategies.notes: Session-specific strategy. U.K. dealers are notorious stop hunters — the initial spike at London open is often a fake-out. The real directional move comes later.

strategies.name: Inside Day Breakout Play

long: Identify pair with daily range contained within prior day's range for at least 2 consecutive days
Buy 10 pips above the high of the previous inside day
short: Same identification: 2+ consecutive inside days
Sell 10 pips below the low of the previous inside day

exit_rules.stop_loss: Stop-and-reverse order for 2 lots placed 10 pips beyond the opposite extreme of the nearest inside day
exit_rules.take_profit: Close position at 2x risk or begin trailing stop at that level
exit_rules.false_breakout_protection: If stop-and-reverse triggered, place new stop 10 pips beyond the opposite side of the nearest inside day

risk_management.position_sizing: Can use stop-and-reverse to capture real direction after false breakout

indicators.name: MACD Histogram
indicators.use: Determine directional bias of breakout (optional optimization)

strategies.further_optimization: Technical formations (ascending/descending triangles) help predict breakout direction. Fibonacci and MA support/resistance below inside days suggests higher probability upside breakout. Works best ahead of major economic releases.
strategies.market_conditions: Best on daily charts. Fewer false breakouts in tighter-range pairs. Can use hourly charts but daily is more reliable.
strategies.notes: Very clean, codifiable strategy. Inside days signal volatility compression preceding a breakout. The stop-and-reverse mechanism handles false breakouts well.

strategies.name: The Fader (False Breakout Fade)
best_pairs: Less volatile pairs with narrower ranges

long: 14-period ADX < 35 (ideally trending downward)
Wait for market to break BELOW previous day's low by at least 15 pips
Then enter buy 15 pips ABOVE previous day's high (fade the false breakout)
short: 14-period ADX < 35 (ideally trending downward)
Wait for move ABOVE previous day's high by at least 15 pips
Then enter sell 15 pips BELOW previous day's low

strategies.market_conditions: Works best when no significant economic data is scheduled. Avoid using ahead of NFP or similar high-impact releases. Best with less volatile pairs.
strategies.notes: Variation of the Real Deal strategy applied to daily ranges. Requires the market to show a failed breakout in one direction before entering in the opposite direction. Clean rules.

strategies.name: Filtering False Breakouts (20-Day High/Low Strategy)

long: Look for pair making a 20-day high
Wait for pair to reverse over next 3 days and make a 2-day low
Buy if price takes out the 20-day high again within 3 days of the 2-day low
short: Look for pair making a 20-day low
Wait for pair to reverse over next 3 days and make a 2-day high
Sell if price breaks below the 20-day low within 3 days of the 2-day high

exit_rules.stop_loss: A few pips beyond the original 2-day low (longs) or 2-day high (shorts)
exit_rules.take_profit: 2x risk amount OR trail with 2-bar low/high

strategies.market_conditions: Designed for strongly trending markets. Filters out weak breakouts by requiring a pullback and re-break. Produced 722-pip winner in GBP/USD example.
strategies.notes: Very similar to Turtle Trading concept but with a pullback filter. Ensures you enter only after weaker players have been shaken out. High-quality swing trade setup.

strategies.name: Channel Breakout Strategy
best_pairs: Any pair forming a visible channel

setup: Identify a narrow channel on intraday or daily chart (parallel trend lines containing price)
Enter long when price breaks above upper channel line
Enter short when price breaks below lower channel line

exit_rules.stop_loss: Just inside the channel line (10 pips under upper line for longs, 10 pips above lower line for shorts)
exit_rules.trailing_stop: Trail stop as price moves in favor

risk_management.position_sizing: Place orders on both sides of channel

strategies.market_conditions: Common during Asian session with breakout in London or U.S. session. Even more effective when timed with major economic releases. Check if channel forms near expected data release.
strategies.notes: Simple visual strategy. Channels form frequently in forex. Asian session consolidation followed by London/NY breakout is a classic pattern.

strategies.name: Perfect Order Moving Average Strategy

long: Moving averages in perfect order: 10-SMA > 20-SMA > 50-SMA > 100-SMA > 200-SMA
ADX > 20 and pointing upward
Buy 5 candles after initial formation of perfect order (if it still holds)
short: Inverse perfect order: 200-SMA > 100-SMA > 50-SMA > 20-SMA > 10-SMA
ADX > 20 and pointing upward
Sell 5 candles after initial formation

exit_rules.stop_loss: Low of day of initial crossover (longs) / High of day (shorts)
exit_rules.exit_signal: Perfect order no longer holds (10-SMA crosses below 20-SMA for longs)

strategies.market_conditions: Perfect orders are rare events. Strategy aims to capture beginning of major trends. Hold times can be weeks to months.
strategies.notes: Captures major trend moves. 550-pip winner in EUR/USD, 443-pip in USD/CAD examples. Rare setups but very high reward potential.

strategies.name: News Trading — Proactive Strategy
markets: All major pairs affected by the specific release
best_pairs: Pair containing the currency of the releasing country

long: Form a view on upcoming economic data based on leading indicators
Enter position no more than 20 minutes before major news release
Stop: 10 pips below the 2-hour range low OR 30 pips below entry (whichever is closer)
short: Same but opposite direction
Stop: 10 pips above range high OR 30 pips above entry

exit_rules.take_profit_1: Close half when profit = amount risked
exit_rules.take_profit_2: Trail remainder by 20-day SMA on 5-min chart or 3x risk hard stop
exit_rules.breakeven: Move to breakeven after first target

risk_management.risk_reward_ratio: 1:1 on first half, 1:3+ on trailing half

strategies.market_conditions: Requires ability to forecast economic data. Enter before spreads widen. Best when using leading indicators to predict data direction.
strategies.notes: Requires fundamental analysis skill to predict data. Risk-reward is excellent when correct. The 20-minute-before-entry timing avoids spread widening.

strategies.name: News Trading — Reactive Strategy
best_pairs: Pair containing currency of releasing country

rule: Wait for major news to be released
Only trade if surprise is > 100% of market forecast
Enter 5 minutes after the release (allows initial spike to settle)
Stop at the low of the news candle (longs) or high (shorts)

exit_rules.take_profit_1: Close half when profit = amount risked
exit_rules.take_profit_2: Trail remainder by 20-day SMA on 5-min chart or 3x risk
exit_rules.breakeven: Move to breakeven after first target

risk_management.risk_reward_ratio: 1:1 on first half, more on trailing half
risk_management.key_filter: Only trade when surprise > 100% of consensus forecast

strategies.market_conditions: No guesswork on data direction. Larger stops than proactive. May take hours for first target. Follow-through required after initial spike.
strategies.notes: Safer than proactive because no need to predict data. The 100% surprise filter is a good quality screen. Can be combined with proactive for a hybrid approach.

strategies.name: News Trading — Combined Proactive + Reactive

entry_rules.step1: Enter HALF position 20 minutes before release (proactive component)
entry_rules.step2: Stop: 10 pips beyond 2-hour range extreme or 30 pips (whichever smaller)
entry_rules.step3_if_correct: Enter second half 5 minutes after release. Move stop for entire position to 45 pips from second entry.
entry_rules.step3_if_wrong: Exit first position entirely. Do NOT take second position.

exit_rules.take_profit_1: Close half of combined position when profit = 45 pips
exit_rules.take_profit_2: Trail remainder by 20-day SMA on 5-min chart

strategies.notes: Best of both worlds — smaller position risk pre-release, add only if confirmed. If wrong, max loss is only 30 pips on half size.

strategies.name: 20-100 Short-Term Momentum Strategy

long: Price crosses ABOVE both 20-EMA and 100-SMA by 15 pips
MACD histogram has turned positive within last 5 candles
Buy at market
short: Price crosses BELOW both 20-EMA and 100-SMA by 15 pips
MACD histogram has turned negative within last 5 candles
Sell at market

exit_rules.stop_loss: Low of the candle that broke MAs (longs) / High of candle (shorts)
exit_rules.breakeven: Move stop to breakeven after first target

risk_management.risk_reward_ratio: 1:1 on first half, trailing on second

indicators.name: MACD
indicators.use: momentum filter — must turn within 5 bars

strategies.market_conditions: Works during active sessions (London, NY). The 15-pip buffer above/below MAs filters noise. Small stops make losses bearable.
strategies.notes: One of the best-defined strategies in the book. Clean mechanical rules with exact indicator parameters. Tight risk with good reward potential. MACD 5-bar filter ensures fresh momentum.

strategies.name: High-Probability Turn (Extension Fade) Strategy

long: Look for 7 consecutive days where close < open (bearish candles)
Buy at 5:00 PM NY time (next candle open)
short: Look for 7 consecutive days where close > open (bullish candles)
Sell at 5:00 PM NY time

exit_rules.breakeven: Move stop to entry after first target

risk_management.risk_reward_ratio: 1:2 on first half, 1:4 on second half
risk_management.re_entry: If 7-day trade fails, take trade again on 8th day. Math works out positive as long as move doesn't extend past 11th day.

statistical_basis.EUR/USD: 10 instances of 7-day extension in 10 years. Only 5 extended to 8th day. Max was 10 days.
statistical_basis.GBP/USD: 12 instances of 7-day extension. Only 7 extended to 8th day. Max was 12 days.
statistical_basis.USD/JPY: 12 instances of 7-day extension. Only 7 extended to 8th day. Max was 9 days.

strategies.market_conditions: Statistical mean-reversion play. Works because extended moves become increasingly unlikely. For yen crosses, use 8-day trigger instead of 7.
strategies.notes: Backed by 10 years of empirical data. One of the most statistically grounded strategies in the book. The re-entry math is elegant — even with consecutive failures, the strategy recovers. Adjust trigger days for different pairs.

strategies.name: Leveraged Carry Trade

core: Buy the currency with the higher interest rate
Sell the currency with the lower interest rate
Confirm risk aversion is LOW (investors willing to take risk)
Check bond yield spreads are not at extremes
Verify low-yielding currency economy is not improving

exit_when: Risk aversion spikes (global crisis, geopolitical shock)

risk_management.key_risk: Sharp risk-aversion spikes can reverse gains in days (e.g., 1998 LTCM, 9/11)

strategies.market_conditions: Works in low-volatility, risk-on environments. Loses badly during financial crises, risk-off events, and safe-haven flows. 2.5% yield differential = 25% return on 10x leverage.
strategies.notes: Not a short-term strategy. Requires monitoring macro risk environment. Works beautifully in calm markets but can reverse violently. JPY carry trade is the classic example.

strategies.name: Bond Spread as Leading Indicator for FX

core: Track 10-year government bond yield differentials between pair currencies
When yield spread widens in favor of a currency, buy that currency
When yield spread narrows, expect currency weakness
Calculate spread: base currency yield minus quote currency yield

exit_rules.exit_when: Yield spread trend reverses or reaches extreme levels

risk_management.use_as: Leading indicator to confirm or filter trades from other strategies

strategies.market_conditions: Works better over longer time horizons. Interest rate differentials tend to lead currency prices. Best used as a confirmation tool alongside other strategies.
strategies.notes: Not a standalone trading system but a powerful filter/confirmation tool. Track yield spreads in Excel and overlay with currency charts. Empirical data from 2002-2005 supports leading indicator relationship.

additional_concepts.name: Multiple Time Frame Analysis
additional_concepts.description: Use daily charts for trend direction, hourly for entry timing, 15-min for day trade entries. Always trade in direction of higher timeframe trend.

additional_concepts.name: ADX Environment Classification
additional_concepts.description: ADX < 20 = range-bound (use oscillators). ADX > 25 = trending (use trend-following). Check before every trade.

additional_concepts.name: Risk Reversal Sentiment Indicator
additional_concepts.description: Track 25-delta option risk reversals. Extreme readings (1 std dev from mean) signal contrarian opportunities. Near-zero = range-bound market.

additional_concepts.name: Option Volatility Breakout/Range Signal
additional_concepts.description: When 1-month implied volatility drops well below 3-month volatility, expect breakout. When 1-month is above 3-month, expect range reversion.

additional_concepts.name: Commodity-Currency Correlation Trading
additional_concepts.description: AUD/USD and NZD/USD correlate 0.80 with gold. USD/CAD inversely correlates -0.40 with oil. Gold/oil trends can lead currency moves.

additional_concepts.name: Seasonality Patterns
additional_concepts.description: January: USD strengthens vs EUR (82%), CHF (82%), JPY (73%). May: AUD/NZD weaken. July: USD/JPY and USD/CAD strengthen. August: reverses July. September: USD weakens vs CHF/GBP. November: NZD strengthens.

additional_concepts.name: Strong Economy / Weak Economy Pairing
additional_concepts.description: When expressing a view, always pair the strongest economy's currency against the weakest. Magnifies moves and extends trends.

additional_concepts.name: Intervention Trading
additional_concepts.description: Central bank intervention creates 100-250 pip moves in minutes. Watch for verbal warnings. Trade WITH intervention, not against it. Use tight trailing stops (15-20 pips) to lock in profits.

session_trading_insights.asian_session: Quietest. Tight ranges. Good for channel formation. GBP/JPY and AUD/JPY most active.
session_trading_insights.london_session: Most active (42% of volume). Real directional moves begin. Stop-hunting common at open.
session_trading_insights.us_session: Second most active (19%). Overlap with London (8-11 AM NY) is peak liquidity.
session_trading_insights.overlap_europe_us: 8:00 AM - 11:00 AM NY. Highest activity. Best for breakout strategies.
session_trading_insights.avoid: 16:00-20:00 GMT generally quiet. Weekend gaps possible.

stop_placement_methods: Two-day low method (10 pips below 2-day low)
risk_management_rules.breakeven_rule: Move stop to breakeven once profit equals initial risk amount
risk_management_rules.scaling: Close half at first target, trail remainder

meta.book_focus: Forex-specific with session-based, fundamental, and technical strategies
meta.unique_value: Combines microstructure insights (order flow, round numbers, stop hunting) with clean mechanical rules. One of few books providing exact entry/exit rules for forex strategies.
meta.overall_quality: High — strategies are well-defined with specific rules, parameters, and worked examples. Statistical backing provided for extension move strategy.
